title: Payment service p99 latency spike to 12s
symptoms: Payment service p99 latency climbed from 380ms to 12000ms over 8 minutes. Error rate followed, reaching 23%. Database connection count at 98/100.
root_cause: Database connection pool exhaustion caused by a slow query introduced in v3.42.1 that performed a full table scan on the transactions table. Each slow query held a connection for 30+ seconds, exhausting the pool.
resolution: Rolled back to v3.41.0 using kubectl rollout undo. Latency returned to normal within 3 minutes. Added index on transactions.created_at to prevent recurrence.
contributing_factors: Pool size too small for load

title: Order service 45% error rate spike
symptoms: Order service error rate jumped from 0.5% to 47% within 2 minutes. NullPointerException in OrderProcessor.process() visible in logs. Payment service showing cascade errors.
root_cause: Null pointer exception in OrderProcessor when discount_code field was null — new validation code assumed non-null. Affected all orders without a discount code (majority of traffic).
resolution: Deployed hotfix v2.15.1 with null check added. Error rate dropped to baseline within 4 minutes of hotfix deployment.

title: API gateway CPU saturation — 97%
symptoms: API gateway CPU climbed from 28% to 97% over 15 minutes. Request latency increased 3x. No corresponding traffic spike observed.
root_cause: ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) in the request validation middleware. A malformed request from a bot triggered catastrophic backtracking in a regex pattern.
resolution: Added input length limit of 1000 characters before regex evaluation. Rewritten regex to avoid catastrophic backtracking. Rate-limited the attacking IP.

title: Notification service OOM — memory grew to 98%
symptoms: Notification service memory grew from 55% to 98% over 35 minutes. Service OOM killed at 98%. 12000 undelivered notifications.
root_cause: Unbounded in-memory queue accumulating notification jobs. A downstream email provider was rate-limiting us, causing jobs to queue without being processed. No queue size limit was set.
resolution: Added max queue size of 10000 with backpressure. Configured dead-letter queue for overflow. Increased rate limit with email provider. Service restarted cleanly.
contributing_factors: No circuit breaker for email provider

title: Payment service DB connection pool exhaustion
symptoms: Payment service DB connections at 100/100. Requests timing out waiting for connection. Error rate 35%. Connection wait time > 30s.
root_cause: Connection leak introduced by a refactor that removed the database connection cleanup in a finally block. Under load, connections accumulated and were never returned to pool.
resolution: Deployed v4.1.2 with connection leak fix (added finally block). Existing leaked connections freed by service restart. Pool stabilized within 5 minutes.

title: Order service Kafka consumer lag — 45k messages
symptoms: Kafka consumer group order-processor lag grew to 45000 messages over 20 minutes. Order processing delay grew to 8 minutes. No consumer crashes observed.
root_cause: External inventory API latency increased from 50ms to 2000ms due to a deployment on their side. Each order message required an inventory check, so processing time increased 40x.
resolution: Added timeout of 500ms for inventory API calls. Introduced fallback to last-known-good inventory cache. Consumer lag caught up within 20 minutes.
contributing_factors: No timeout on external API call

title: User service errors after bad deployment
symptoms: User service error rate jumped to 38% within 3 minutes of deployment v5.2.0. Errors were configuration-related: new environment variable JWT_SECRET not set in production.
root_cause: Deployment v5.2.0 added a required JWT_SECRET environment variable that was set in staging but not in production secrets. Service started but failed all auth requests.
resolution: Added JWT_SECRET to production Kubernetes secrets. Rolled out updated deployment. Error rate dropped immediately.

title: Payment service latency spike — database index dropped
symptoms: Payment service p99 latency spiked to 15000ms. Database queries taking 10-12s each. No deployment in last 4 hours.
root_cause: An automated database maintenance script accidentally dropped a critical index on the payments table during its scheduled run at 3am.
resolution: Recreated the index: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_payments_user_created ON payments(user_id, created_at). Latency returned to normal in 8 minutes.
contributing_factors: Maintenance window at peak EU hours

title: API gateway memory leak — long-running connections
symptoms: API gateway memory grew 1% every 5 minutes over 6 hours. Restarted by watchdog at 92% memory. 3000 in-flight WebSocket connections lost.
root_cause: WebSocket connections were stored in a global map but never removed when clients disconnected abnormally. Over hours, millions of dead references accumulated.
resolution: Added cleanup for dead WebSocket connections (heartbeat + TTL). Fixed connection deregistration on all disconnect paths.
contributing_factors: No connection cleanup on abnormal disconnect

title: Order service error spike — inventory service outage
symptoms: Order service error rate 28%. Errors: 'inventory-service: connection refused'. inventory-service had been OOM killed and was in CrashLoopBackoff.
root_cause: inventory-service OOM killed due to memory leak, entered CrashLoopBackoff. Order service had no circuit breaker and failed synchronously when inventory was unreachable.
resolution: Restarted inventory-service after increasing memory limit to 2Gi. Added circuit breaker in order-service for inventory calls. Added fallback to allow orders with 'inventory pending' status.

title: CPU saturation on payment service — traffic spike
symptoms: Payment service CPU at 95%. Latency 5x normal. Traffic spike due to Valentine's Day promotion. HPA had not yet scaled.
root_cause: Organic traffic surge 4x normal from marketing campaign. HPA scale-up lagged 8 minutes behind load. CPU saturated during lag period.
resolution: Manually scaled to 12 replicas. CPU dropped to 45%. Added pre-scaling rule for known marketing events.

title: Notification service Kafka consumer crash loop
symptoms: Kafka consumer group notification-sender lag growing to 80000 messages. Consumer repeatedly crashing and rejoining group, causing constant rebalances.
root_cause: A malformed message was published to the notifications topic containing invalid JSON. The consumer crashed on deserialization, restarted, consumed the same poison message, and crashed again.
resolution: Added schema validation with dead-letter queue routing for invalid messages. Manually skipped the poison message by advancing offset. Consumer stabilized.

title: User service latency — Redis connection pool
symptoms: User service p99 latency spiked to 2000ms. Redis connection wait time high. Redis connections at max.
root_cause: Redis maxconn limit was hit as user-service replicas were scaled up without adjusting Redis client pool per instance. Total connections = replicas × pool_size exceeded Redis limit.
resolution: Reduced per-instance Redis pool size from 50 to 10. Total connections dropped to acceptable level. Latency returned to baseline.
contributing_factors: Pool size not adjusted for horizontal scaling

title: Inventory service DB connection exhaustion post-migration
symptoms: Inventory service DB connections at 100/100. Requests failing with connection timeout. No traffic spike.
root_cause: Database migration ran as part of deployment and held a lock on the inventory table for 12 minutes. Application connections accumulated waiting for the migration lock.
resolution: Migration completed, lock released, connections drained. Added migration timeout guard and connection monitoring at 80% threshold.

title: API gateway 502 errors — payment service timeout
symptoms: API gateway returning 502 Bad Gateway on /payments endpoints. Payment service was healthy but slow. Gateway timeout set to 5s, payment service averaging 7s.
root_cause: Payment service latency increased due to an upstream banking API being slow. API gateway timeout (5s) was shorter than the banking API timeout (30s), causing 502s before payment completed.
resolution: Increased API gateway timeout for /payments to 35s. Added async payment processing with webhook callback for long-running payments.

title: Notification service memory growth — email attachment buffering
symptoms: Notification service memory growing 5% per hour. No memory freed between GC cycles.
root_cause: Email attachments were being loaded entirely into memory before sending. A large PDF batch job (500MB) triggered runaway memory growth.
resolution: Implemented streaming attachment processing. Added per-attachment size limit of 10MB. Large attachments now chunked.
contributing_factors: Batch job ran during business hours

title: Payment service CPU saturation — cryptographic operations
symptoms: Payment service CPU at 98%. All activity in crypto library. Traffic normal.
root_cause: RSA key size was changed from 2048 to 4096 bit in new deployment. Encryption/decryption CPU cost increased 8x. Service became CPU-bound.
resolution: Rolled back to 2048-bit keys. Scheduled key rotation during off-peak hours with performance testing first.
contributing_factors: No performance testing for crypto change

title: Order service cascading failure from inventory outage
symptoms: inventory-service offline (node failure). order-service error rate 60%. payment-service error rate 15% due to order failures.
root_cause: Underlying VM hosting inventory-service failed. Single point of failure with no replica. Order-service had no fallback.
resolution: inventory-service restarted on new node. Added second replica with anti-affinity rules. Added circuit breaker and cache fallback in order-service.

title: User service Kafka lag — consumer thread blocked
symptoms: user-events consumer lag growing. Consumer alive but not processing. Thread dump showed consumer thread blocked on database call.
root_cause: Consumer thread blocked waiting for a database query that was holding a lock (a maintenance vacuum ran concurrently). Since Kafka poll() wasn't called within max.poll.interval.ms, consumer was kicked from group.
resolution: Made database calls async in consumer (non-blocking). Added `max.poll.interval.ms=300000`. Ensured vacuum runs in maintenance window.

title: API gateway latency — TLS certificate renewal delay
symptoms: API gateway p99 latency spiked to 3000ms at midnight. TLS handshake errors in logs. Certificate had 1 day remaining.
root_cause: TLS certificate renewal process triggered at midnight, requiring all connections to re-negotiate. Under load, TLS handshakes queued and caused latency.
resolution: Certificate renewal completed within 5 minutes. Added renewal to off-peak maintenance window (3am). Added certificate expiry monitoring at 14-day threshold.
